---
title: "Creating A New Process Workflow"
---

---
title: "Creating a New Process Workflow"
source: "https://support.hyland.com/r/Current/Hyland-Intelligent-Document-Processing-IDP/vqu1745904704384"
section: "Hyland Intelligent Document Processing (IDP)"
---

The document processing platform enables you to create a workflow for your IDP solution that you configured in the Configuring Document AI section. You can create your own process flow template or choose one from the pre-built templates.

1.  Navigate to Studio Modeler > All IDP projects and select the project for which you want to build a process workflow. The Class page is displayed.  Note: You need to complete configuring the project before creating a workflow.
2.  On the Class page, click Build Process. The Select a template to start IDP processing dialog box is displayed.  Note: You can also click the ellipsis button and select Process > New from template.  ![](data:image/gif;base64,)
-  Create from Scratch: This IDP template enables you to create the process workflow for your project from the beginning.
-  Document Classification Only: This IDP template provides the framework for the document classification process workflow.
-  Document Classification & Field Extraction: This IDP template provides the framework for the document classification and field extraction process workflow.
-  Document Separation and Classification: This IDP template provides the framework for the document separation and document classification process workflow.
-  Document Separation, Classification and Extraction: This IDP template provides the framework for the document separation, classification and field extraction process workflow.
-  Intelligent Document Processing with Federated Content Connection: This IDP template provides the framework for adding a content federation connector to an IDP process workflow. This enables you to send documents processed by IDP to your connected OnBase instance.
3.  Hover the cursor on the Create from Scratch tile and then click Start Building. The Document AI Process area is displayed.   ![](data:image/gif;base64,)
4.  Create an end event and connect it with the start event.   ![](data:image/gif;base64,)
5.  Select the start event and in the Properties panel, assign the relevant document upload form and process variable.    ![](data:image/gif;base64,)
For more information on forms and process variables, see Creating Process Variables and Creating a Document Upload Form.

6.  Drag and & drop the Document Classification connector into the canvas, create a new IDP Connector instance and connect it with the Start event.  Note: The Document Classification process can connect to IDP services through a dedicated connector. There are dedicated connectors for Document Classification and Document Extraction and Document Separation.
7.  Name the connector Document Classification and assign the batchState variable in Output Mapping and the contents variable in Input Mapping.   ![](data:image/gif;base64,)
8.  Add a User Task by dragging and dropping the Create user task element on to the canvas and connecting it with the Document Classification activity. Additionally, you need to complete the following steps:
- Rename the user activity to Class Review.
- In the Properties panel, select the Screen option and in the Screen Name field, select IDP Class Verification.
- Map the Input and Output variables accordingly
- Under Assignment > Expression, click Assign task to a single user and then assign the task to an appropriate user.
![](data:image/gif;base64,)
9.  Drag and drop the Document Extraction connector into the canvas, select the existing IDP Connector instance and connect it with the Class Review activity. Additionally, you need to complete the following step.
- Rename the activity to Document Extraction and map the Input and Output variables accordingly.
![](data:image/gif;base64,)
10.  Click the Create sub process icon ![](data:image/gif;base64,) to add an extraction verification task for each individual document.
11.  Connect the Document Extraction activity with the created sub-process and connect the sub-process with the end-process event. Additionally, you need to complete the following steps in the Property panel.
- Select Sequential in the Multi Instance type field, to have each document verified sequentially.
- To configure how the sub process should loop over the batch information to find the required user tasks, enter the following text in the Collection field batchState.documents.
- In the Element variable field enter documentState
- In the Element index variable field, enter documentIndex
12.  Add a start and an end event element to the sub-process.
13.  Add a user task by dragging and dropping the Create user task element on to the sub process. Additionally, you need to complete the following steps:
- Rename the user activity to Field Review.
- In the Properties panel, select the Screen option and in the Screen Name field, select IDP Field Verification.
- Map the Input and Output variables accordingly.
- Under Assignment > Expression, click Assign task to a single user and then assign the task to an appropriate user.
- To configure the documentIndex process properties, click the Edit icon and under the Expression tab, enter $(documentIndex)
![](data:image/gif;base64,)
14.  Click the Save ![](data:image/gif;base64,) icon to save the process workflow.
15.  Click Validate. The project is screened for validation errors.
16.  Click Release. The Are you sure you want to release a new version of this project dialog box is displayed.
17.  Click Confirm. The current project version is released.

You can now deploy the project. For more information, see Deploying a Project.
